pattern: Every offer only matters at the house where it ends, so scanning houses left to right and asking 'what's the best I could have earned up to just before this offer started, plus this offer's gold' turns the search over exponentially many buyer combinations into one linear pass with a running best-so-far table.
hints: Think of the houses as a number line from 0 to n-1, and imagine sweeping across it left to right while tracking the best total profit achievable using only houses seen so far.

Group the offers by the house index where they end. When your sweep reaches that house, an offer becomes usable, and taking it only requires knowing the best profit achievable strictly before the offer's start house.

Keep a running array of best-profit-so-far indexed by house position; at each house, the best value either carries over unchanged (skip every offer ending here) or improves by combining an ending offer's gold with the best profit recorded at its starting house.

edge_inputs.description: single house, single offer covering it
two small disjoint offers beat one larger overlapping offer
duplicate identical intervals with different gold, must pick the richer one
three disjoint single-house offers, all can be taken
an offer that starts exactly where another ends should chain, not overlap

stress.description: many random overlapping offers over a mid-sized house range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    m = rng.randint(1, min(n * 2, 100000))
    offers = []
    for _ in range(m):
        s = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        e = rng.randint(s, n - 1)
        g = rng.randint(1, 1000)
        offers.append([s, e, g])
    return [n, offers]
